_metadata.description: Tunisian green funding programs, incentives, and international financing for entrepreneurs

national_programs.name: Fond de Transition Energétique (FTE)
national_programs.managing_body: ANME (Agence Nationale de Maîtrise de l'Energie)
national_programs.description: National fund financing energy efficiency and renewable energy projects. Provides subsidies covering 20-70% of eligible investment costs for energy audits, efficiency upgrades, and renewable energy installations.
conditions: Mandatory energy audit for large consumers (>800 TOE/year)
Project must be validated by ANME
national_programs.how_to_apply: Submit project proposal to ANME regional office with energy audit results and investment plan. ANME reviews and approves within 1-3 months.

national_programs.name: PROSOL (Programme Solaire)
national_programs.description: National program promoting solar water heaters for residential, commercial, and industrial use. Provides subsidy plus subsidized credit mechanism via STEG billing.
national_programs.coverage: Up to 600 TND subsidy per solar water heater (residential). Commercial rates vary. Credit repaid via STEG electricity bill.
conditions: Equipment must be approved by ANME
national_programs.how_to_apply: Through authorized dealers and installers. Subsidy applied at purchase. Credit repaid via STEG bill.

national_programs.name: DecarboAct
national_programs.description: Program supporting industrial enterprises in decarbonization. Provides technical assistance for carbon footprint measurement, energy efficiency, and developing decarbonization roadmaps.
eligible_activities: Technical assistance for clean technology adoption
conditions: Willingness to commit to decarbonization targets
national_programs.how_to_apply: Apply through ANME or GIZ Tunisia office.

national_programs.name: FODEP (Fonds de Dépollution)
national_programs.managing_body: ANPE (Agence Nationale de Protection de l'Environnement)
national_programs.description: National depollution fund providing subsidies for pollution reduction investments. Covers wastewater treatment, air pollution control, and solid waste management.
national_programs.coverage: Up to 20% of eligible investment (can be combined with other incentives)
national_programs.how_to_apply: File application with ANPE. Submit environmental assessment and investment plan.

national_programs.name: Tax Incentives for Renewable Energy
national_programs.description: Various tax deductions and exemptions for investments in renewable energy and energy efficiency equipment.
eligible_activities: Import of renewable energy equipment (customs duty reduction)
national_programs.coverage: Reduced customs duties on renewable energy equipment. Tax deductions on qualifying investments.
national_programs.how_to_apply: Through standard investment declaration process. Consult ANME for approved equipment list.

national_programs.name: Self-Generation Regime (Autoproduction)
national_programs.description: Legal framework allowing businesses to produce their own electricity from renewable sources and sell surplus to STEG grid. Governed by Decree 2009 and subsequent amendments.
national_programs.coverage: Feed-in tariff for surplus electricity. Priority grid connection.
national_programs.how_to_apply: Submit authorization application to Ministry of Energy. Negotiate grid connection with STEG.

international_financing.name: African Development Bank (AfDB) — Tunisia Green Investment
international_financing.description: AfDB has committed approximately $110 million to Tunisia energy transition since 2019. Finances large-scale renewable energy (Kairouan solar, Gabes wind), grid modernization, and energy efficiency through credit lines via local banks.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Access via local partner banks (BFPME, BNA). Preferential rates for green investments.
international_financing.how_to_access: Through Tunisian partner banks offering AfDB green credit lines.

international_financing.name: Green Climate Fund (GCF)
international_financing.description: UN-backed fund supporting developing countries in climate mitigation and adaptation. Tunisia is an eligible recipient. Funds channeled through accredited entities.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Typically finances larger programs. Startups benefit indirectly through national programs funded by GCF.
international_financing.how_to_access: Through GCF Nationally Designated Authority (NDA) in Tunisia — Ministry of Environment.

international_financing.name: Global Environment Facility (GEF)
international_financing.description: Funds projects addressing biodiversity, climate change, land degradation, and international waters. Tunisia has accessed GEF for multiple environmental projects.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Indirect — through national programs. Some GEF small grants program accessible to SMEs and NGOs.
international_financing.how_to_access: Through UNDP Tunisia or Ministry of Environment (GEF Operational Focal Point).

international_financing.name: EU SwitchMed / SWITCH to Green
international_financing.description: EU-funded program supporting Mediterranean countries in transitioning to sustainable consumption and production. Provides training, networking, and green entrepreneurship support in Tunisia.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Highly relevant — specifically targets Mediterranean startups and SMEs for green business models.
international_financing.how_to_access: Apply through SwitchMed open calls. Check switchmed.eu for current opportunities.

international_financing.name: European Investment Bank (EIB) — FEMIP
international_financing.description: EIB's Facility for Euro-Mediterranean Investment and Partnership provides financing for private sector development and infrastructure in Mediterranean partner countries including Tunisia.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Access via local partner banks. Green investment credit lines available.
international_financing.how_to_access: Through Tunisian partner banks (Amen Bank, BIAT, etc.) offering EIB credit lines.

international_financing.name: KfW / AFD Tunisia Green Lines
international_financing.description: German (KfW) and French (AFD) development banks provide green credit lines through Tunisian banks for energy efficiency, renewable energy, and environmental investments.
typical_instruments: Subsidized credit lines via local banks
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Green credit at preferential rates through local partner banks.
international_financing.how_to_access: Through Tunisian partner banks offering KfW/AFD green credit lines (BFPME, Amen Bank, BIAT, BNA).

international_financing.name: EBRD Green Economy Transition (GET)
international_financing.description: EBRD supports Tunisia's green economy transition with financing and advisory for businesses investing in green technologies and sustainability measures.
international_financing.relevance_for_startups: Growing EBRD presence in Tunisia. Green finance for SMEs via partner banks.
international_financing.how_to_access: Direct application for larger projects, or via local partner banks for SME financing.

for_all_startups: Apply for FTE subsidies for energy efficiency/solar
Explore tax incentives for renewable energy equipment
Check EU SwitchMed for green entrepreneurship support
for_manufacturing: Apply for FODEP for pollution reduction
